name: Stuffed Pistolettes
recipeInstructions:
1. Hollow out the buns from the bottom.
2. Beat egg and water together and brush inside and outside of each bun.
3. Bake in a 350-degree oven until brown and crisp. Remove and let cool.
4. Melt butter in a skillet and add minced garlic. Simmer on low heat for five minutes.
5. Brush each bun with the garlic butter mixture and sprinkle with cheese. Set aside.
6. Dice chicken into bite-size pieces. Saute chicken and shrimp in 1/3 cup butter until cooked through. Remove from skillet and drain well.
7. In the same skillet, add remaining butter and saute onions, celery, and minced garlic. Season with salt and pepper. Remove and drain well.
8. Combine all drained ingredients back into the skillet. Add diced mushrooms and sherry. Stir thoroughly.
9. Prepare the sauce by melting butter over low heat. Gradually add flour, salt, and pepper, stirring until smooth. Slowly add milk, stirring constantly until thick and creamy. Remove from heat.
10. Add both cheeses and Worcestershire sauce to the sauce. Mix well until cheeses are melted.
11. Stir in the sauce into the chicken mixture until well mixed.
12. Spoon the mixture into individual bread loaves and warm in a broiler for five minutes. Serve immediately. Garnish with chopped parsley if desired.
13. Serves 8.
